title: Seed
description: Structured JSON intermediate representation (IR) distilled from a grill or conversation session. Version 4: open_threads changes from string[] to object array with id/text/first_seen_iteration. Adds disposed_threads (lock list), iteration counter, and verification stamp.

schema_version.description: Always '4'. Hard cutover from v3 — old files fail validation by design.

producer.description: The skill that generated this seed. Typically 'to-seed'.

source.description: What this seed was derived from. Null for seeds generated directly from a conversation. {type:'seed', ref:<path>} for Mode-2 handoff-merge seeds that fold a prior session's resolutions onto a parent seed.

title.description: Short human-readable title for this topic.

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier derived from title, max ~40 chars.

summary.description: One paragraph: what was discussed, what was decided, current readiness state.

status.description: Derived from open_threads: 'ready' when open_threads is empty, 'draft' otherwise. Never hand-set. to-tasks hard-refuses a draft seed.

iteration.description: Number of grill/verification iterations this seed has gone through. Documentation and telemetry only — does not gate any pipeline step.

decisions.description: Resolved decisions. Each entry is a concrete resolved statement. Empty array if none.

id.description: Stable identifier for this thread (e.g. 'ot-001'). Never reused, even after disposal.

text.description: The unresolved question or judgment call.

first_seen_iteration.description: Iteration number when this thread was first recorded.

open_threads.description: Genuinely unresolved JUDGMENT only — questions where the answer changes what gets built and no consensus was reached. Empty array when status is 'ready'.

id.description: Stable identifier matching the original open_threads entry.

text.description: The original thread text, preserved verbatim.

disposition.description: How the thread was resolved. 'decided': a concrete resolution was reached. 'deferred': parked for a future session. 'rejected': explicitly ruled out of scope.

iteration.description: Iteration number when this thread was disposed.

disposed_threads.description: Lock list of threads that have been disposed. Grill resume mode treats these ids as off-limits — they cannot be re-raised under any name (relabel-resurrection blocked).

iteration.description: Iteration number at which verification ran.

personas.description: Names of adversary persona agents that ran (e.g. ['grounding', 'accuracy', 'completeness', 'coherence']).

refutations_upheld.description: Count of refutations the judge panel upheld (2-of-3 majority). Zero means a clean pass.

clean.description: True when refutations_upheld === 0. Convenience flag for quick gate checks.

status.description: 'verified': adversary panel completed and seed passed or corrections were applied. 'degraded': panel did not fully complete (agent error/timeout); to-tasks hard-refuses degraded seeds.

refutations_screened.description: Count of refutations the round-1 batched screener marked rejected, so they never escalated to the 3-judge panel. Observability data for calibrating the escalation threshold.

verification.description: Verification stamp written by to-seed after the adversary panel run. Absent on v3 and earlier seeds (backward-compatible: to-tasks skips the check when the field is missing).

text.description: Short description of the deferred item.

rationale.description: Why this is deferred rather than resolved or rejected.

context.description: Background needed to pick this up in a future session.

source_seed.description: Path to the seed that introduced this deferred item, or null if originated here.

deferred.description: Work items acknowledged but explicitly set aside for a future effort. Each item carries enough context to be revisited independently.

next_action.description: The single most important next step. Not a list.

evidence.description: One or two sentences: data, complaints, or research that motivated this.

user_stories.description: Minimum 5 entries if included. Format: As a <actor>, I want <feature>, so that <benefit>.

implementation_decisions.description: Modules, interfaces, schema, APIs decided. Implementation details that are decided but conditional on runtime conditions belong here or in task acceptance criteria, not in open_threads.

text.description: Short description of the explicitly excluded item.

rationale.description: Why this is excluded. Consumed by to-tasks as a negative guard so no task slice is generated for this item.

out_of_scope.description: Items explicitly rejected from scope. Structured as {text, rationale} so to-tasks can read rationale as negative context and skip any slice that would implement a rejected item.

further_notes.description: Miscellaneous notes that don't fit other fields.

condensed_from.description: List of original seed filenames (basenames only) that were merged to produce this condensed seed. Used by tools that resolve backlinks when the original file is no longer present. Optional — omit for non-condensed seeds.

pr_url.description: URL of the pull request this seed was distilled from.

head_branch.description: Head branch of the PR (the branch carrying the changes under review).

thread_id.description: Identifier of the review thread or comment. GraphQL global node id for inline threads, numeric databaseId string for top-level PR comments.

thread_id_type.description: Which id kind thread_id holds, so downstream consumers (e.g. relay) key resolution correctly.

final_class.description: Final class after diligence and the HITL gate.

disposition.description: Final disposition for bug-class items, or null for non-bug items.

reply_body.description: PR-feedback only. Draft reply for this thread, written by pr-revise, grounded in the diligence finding. The reviewer's handle is omitted (the thread already targets them), and no commit SHA is hand-written (relay stitches the fixing commit in at post time). to-tasks copies this verbatim onto the generated reply task's reply_body.

reply_url.description: PR-feedback only. The originating review-comment URL this reply answers (from pr-revise harvest). to-tasks copies it onto the reply task's reply_url.

fix.description: PR-feedback only. The code change to make on THIS PR to satisfy this item, or null when no code change is warranted (question, declined nit, false_flag, or a change deferred to a later PR). When non-null, to-tasks emits a `code` task from this scope plus a `reply` task blocked_by it, so the reply can cite the fixing commit. When null, to-tasks emits a reply task only.

items.description: One entry per harvested feedback item, in harvest order. Carries both the code scope (fix) and the PR response (reply_body) so to-tasks can generate paired code + reply tasks without re-reading the session.

provenance.description: PR-feedback provenance written by the pr-revise pipeline (Step 8). Present only on seeds distilled from PR review threads. Downstream skills (e.g. relay) read items[] to resolve threads. Omit for seeds not produced by revise-pr.

examples.title: Replace session tokens with short-lived JWTs
examples.summary: Current auth stores opaque session tokens in Redis. Legal requires short-lived, auditable tokens. All judgment calls resolved: migrate to RS256 JWTs with 15-min expiry and refresh-token rotation. Redis session store removed after migration.
decisions: Use signed JWTs (RS256) with 15-minute access token expiry
Refresh tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies, rotated on every use
Redis session store is deprecated and removed after migration, not alongside it
No silent refresh in the client — expired token triggers a full re-auth UX

disposed_threads.text: Should refresh token expiry be 7 days or 30 days?

deferred.rationale: Product has not decided the UX for concurrent sessions; deferring until that decision is made.
deferred.context: Current implementation issues one session per user. Multi-device requires either session list UI or silent coexistence strategy.

examples.next_action: Run /to-tasks to generate the migration task breakdown
examples.problem_statement: Opaque session tokens in Redis do not carry claims, cannot be audited externally, and have no expiry short of explicit revocation. Legal has flagged this for compliance.
examples.solution: Replace session tokens with RS256-signed JWTs. Access tokens expire in 15 minutes. Refresh tokens rotate on use and are stored in HttpOnly cookies. Redis session store is removed post-migration.
examples.evidence: Legal audit (2026-05-15) flagged session token storage as non-compliant. Three internal incidents involved stale tokens remaining valid after account deletion.
implementation_decisions: JWT signing uses RS256 with a 2048-bit key pair; private key stored in secrets manager
Refresh token table added to Postgres: columns id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, revoked_at
Auth middleware validates JWT signature and expiry before any handler runs
testing_decisions: Unit tests cover token signing, expiry validation, and refresh rotation logic
Integration tests hit the real Postgres refresh token table, not a mock

out_of_scope.text: Token introspection endpoint for third-party consumers
out_of_scope.rationale: No external consumers exist today; adding this would be speculative scope.

risks_and_tradeoffs: 15-minute expiry shortens the window for revocation but increases refresh round-trips
Dual-read migration period leaves old session tokens valid; any Redis breach during this window is still a risk
further_notes: RS256 key rotation cadence not yet decided — revisit before go-live
